page: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the instance achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core scoring and single-threaded text processing. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 10% of tested systems. However, multi-core scaling shows limitations, with stress-ng multi-core performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, while small-block cached read bandwidth and overall PassMark memory scores are relatively weak.

Given its low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2 and dedicated compute resources, the c8id.xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for single-threaded tasks, light web serving, and applications requiring fast local NVMe storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX supports specialized vector and matrix operations. It is well-suited for single-threaded data processing, lightweight compilation, and database operations that benefit from high-speed local storage, though it is less optimal for memory-intensive or highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, this instance includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded compression and text processing, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks that require fast local storage but have limited memory requirements. It is well-suited for single-threaded data processing, lightweight software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for targeted workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 237 GB NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Optimal for single-threaded data processing, lightweight compilation, and database tasks
